violations.text: Cheese, cauliflower, and shrimp were observed held at 52-54 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit, with an ambient temperature of 54 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff placed foods on ice and/or returned to walk-in during inspection. PIC called for service. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings under equipment near walk-in. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Staff brought to be cleaned and sanitized; staff heated water during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. PIC informed, spoke with staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

A bucket of flour was observed in the hand-washing sink on the cook line. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Technician was on-site; stated part is on order. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Technician arrived during inspection and replaced feed line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
Chemical spray bottle was stored on the same shelf with food/equipment/single service articles in the kitchen. Removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings under equipment near the walk-in. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

Utensils were observed stored in water that was less than 135 degrees between uses. Utensils were brought to be cleaned and sanitized, water replaced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The fryer basket was in poor repair, with peeling laminate. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bar was lacking paper towels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen cookline was blocked by a strainer. The soda hose was observed hanging in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Strainer was removed, and hook for soda hose was relocated to prevent hanging in the sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025
A divider is needed between the hand washing sink and food storage in the kitchen line area. PIC stated work order is scheduled for this week. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Sealed containers of food observed covered with condensate ice in the walk-in freezer. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

The cold drawer unit located in the cook line area was not in good repair, observed holding an ambient temperature of 46 degrees. All foods were moved to alternate refrigeration or placed on ice. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the stand mixer have an accumulation of food residue. Staff were instructed to clean and sanitize during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The following were observed in poor repair: -door to walk-in cooler (hole in door) -closing latch to walk-in freezer. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
A strainer was observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
The hand-washing sink at the kitchen and bar were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

Working containers of various dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

Personal belongings were stored in the "chip bus" in close proximity to food and food storage containers; jackets were hanging on shelving units in dry storage. PIC created temporary separation of personal belongings in the bus and moved jackets to employee coat rack during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
A divider is lacking between hand sinks and food storage on the cook line. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Grill scraper & utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The cold-drawer unit located in the line area was not in good repair, observed with an ambient temperature of 55 degrees. All foods requiring refrigeration were placed in walk-in or on-ice. PIC called for service. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the dish machine. Inspector provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -tabletop mixer -stand mixer -can opener base -gaskets of beverage walk-in. PIC had items cleaned and sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
The door to the walk-in freezer is in poor repair, observed with the closing mechanism loose. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The floor and wall junctures in the dry storage or ice machine area do not have coving or have damaged wall/coving junctures. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025
The beverage drain tubing receptacle in the walk-in was observed wrapped with plastic wrap. A permanent covering is needed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The tables located in the dining area were not in good repair. Inspector observed tables pitted which is no longer easily cleanable, durable and porous. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023
